---
output:
  md_document:
    variant: gfm
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
helper <- "vignettes/real-example-helpers.R"
source(helper)
if (!bt_real_examples_enabled()) {
  stop(
    "Render index.Rmd with BLUERTOPO_BUILD_REAL_EXAMPLES=true for the pkgdown homepage.",
    call. = FALSE
  )
}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/home-",
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5,
  dpi = 144
)
```

# bluertopo

<img src="man/figures/logo.png" align="right" height="139" alt="bluertopo logo" />

[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/el-cordero/bluer-topo/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/el-cordero/bluer-topo/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![pkgdown](https://github.com/el-cordero/bluer-topo/actions/workflows/pkgdown.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/el-cordero/bluer-topo/actions/workflows/pkgdown.yaml)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg)](https://github.com/el-cordero/bluer-topo/blob/main/LICENSE.md)

`bluertopo` discovers, downloads, verifies, and opens NOAA BlueTopo bathymetry
for an area of interest using `terra`. The package keeps source GeoTIFF and RAT
sidecar files intact by default, records query and catalog provenance, and makes
native source-resolution choices explicit.

BlueTopo is not for navigation. This package performs no vertical-datum
conversion and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or supported by NOAA.

Reference: NOAA, [BlueTopo](https://nauticalcharts.noaa.gov/data/bluetopo.html)
and [BlueTopo specifications](https://nauticalcharts.noaa.gov/data/bluetopo_specs.html).

## Real NOAA Proof

```{r real-proof-setup, include=FALSE}
library(terra)

real <- bt_real_example_setup()
real_aoi <- real$aoi
real_tiles <- real$tiles
real_manifest <- bt_real_download_assets(real)
real_bathy <- bluertopo(
  real_aoi,
  layers = "elevation",
  resolution = "native",
  coverage = "fill",
  details = TRUE,
  progress = FALSE,
  quiet = TRUE
)
```

Example AOI: New York Harbor, centered on Lower Manhattan, the Upper Bay,
Governors Island, and the East River mouth. This homepage proof uses actual NOAA
BlueTopo source tiles downloaded during the pkgdown build.

```{r proof-map, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Actual NOAA BlueTopo source data: New York Harbor elevation with hillshade, contours, and AOI outline.", fig.alt="Hillshaded real NOAA BlueTopo bathymetry map for New York Harbor with contour lines and the AOI outline."}
bt_plot_bathy_map(real_bathy$data, real_aoi, main = "New York Harbor NOAA BlueTopo bathymetry")
```

```{r proof-tiles, echo=FALSE}
tile_table <- bt_tile_table(real_tiles)
bt_display_table(tile_table[c(
  "tile_id",
  "resolution_m",
  "utm_zone",
  "intersection_fraction",
  "selection_reason"
)])
```

```{r proof-manifest, echo=FALSE}
manifest_table <- bt_manifest_table(real_manifest)
bt_display_table(manifest_table[c(
  "tile_id",
  "asset_type",
  "status",
  "verified",
  "downloaded_mb",
  "actual_sha256"
)])
```

## Basic Workflow

```r
library(bluertopo)
library(terra)

aoi <- vect("my_area.gpkg")
bathy <- bluertopo(aoi)
plot(bathy)
```

## Provenance Workflow

```r
result <- bluertopo(aoi, details = TRUE)

result$tiles
result$downloads
result$coverage
result$provenance
```

## Examples

The [Examples tab](articles/examples.html) is rendered from actual NOAA
BlueTopo source tiles for New York Harbor. Normal package tests use small
synthetic fixtures so checks remain network-free. The mixed-grid example uses a
documented secondary real AOI near Key West and Boca Chica Channel because the
current New York Harbor plan is one compatible 4 m native grid.

- [Example gallery](articles/examples.html)
- [Discover tiles and coverage](articles/example-discover-tiles.html)
- [Download original assets](articles/example-download-assets.html)
- [Extract elevation with terra](articles/example-extract-elevation.html)
- [Compare resolution policies](articles/example-resolution-policies.html)
- [Mixed grids and output grid](articles/example-mixed-grids.html)
- [Layers and RAT metadata](articles/example-layers-rat.html)
